NewStrings.LOC_FN_HouseImperialCity: Buy a house in the Imperial City
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseImperialCity_STAGE0_LOG0: I just bought a shack in the Imperial City. It's in the Waterfront district, on the far side of the harbor wall.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseImperialCity_STAGE1_LOG0: Now that I've arrived at my new shack in the Imperial City, I may want to head over to Sergius Verus at Three Brothers Trade Goods to secure some furnishings for it.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseImperialCity_STAGE2_LOG0: I've now bought everything Sergius has for my shack in the Imperial City.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MG17Ambush_STAGE0_LOG0: I need to travel to the south of Cyrodiil and meet a group of Battlemages preparing to storm the ruins of Silorn.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MG17Ambush_STAGE1_LOG0: I have arrived at Silorn, and spoken with Thalfin. We cannot enter the ruins, so I must help prepare an ambush for the Necromancers when they attempt to leave.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MG17Ambush_STAGE2_LOG0: I have been discovered by the Necromancers at Silorn. They will surely have alerted Falcar; I'll need to enter the ruins and find him.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MG17Ambush_STAGE2_LOG1: Falcar has fled back into the ruins of Silorn. I must find him.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MG17Ambush_STAGE3_LOG0: I've recovered the Black Soul Gem. It needs to be taken back to Hannibal Traven at the Arcane University.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MG17Ambush_STAGE4_LOG0: The Black Soul Gem has been delivered to Hannibal Traven.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DABoethia_STAGE0_LOG0: I have been told by one of Boethia's followers that in order to summon the Daedra, I must give a daedra heart as an offering to the statue. [You must be level 2 to begin this quest.]
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DABoethia_STAGE1_LOG0: Boethia has told me that I am to participate in the Tournament of Ten Bloods. When I am ready, I should enter the nearby portal to Oblivion, and I will be sent to one of his realms for the battle.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DABoethia_STAGE2_LOG0: I have defeated all of Boethia's followers in the Tournament of Ten Bloods. I should speak once more with the Daedra Lord.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DABoethia_STAGE3_LOG0: Boethia was pleased with my performance in the Tournament of Ten Bloods. He has rewarded me with the enchanted sword, Goldbrand.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAClavicusVile_STAGE0_LOG0: One of Clavicus Vile's worshippers has told me that in order to summon the Daedra, an offering of 500 gold must be given to the statue. [You must be level 2 to begin this quest.]
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAClavicusVile_STAGE1_LOG0: When I gave my offering at the Shrine of Clavicus Vile, the Daedra Lord told me about Umbra, and how he wishes me to return to him the Umbra Sword. I should start my search in Pell's Gate.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAClavicusVile_STAGE2_LOG0: Barbas, the Hound of Clavicus Vile, has spoken to me through the figurine that appeared in my pack. He tells me that returning the Umbra Sword to Clavicus Vile is a mistake, likely to cause great ruin to Vile's domain.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAClavicusVile_STAGE3_LOG0: I've been told that Umbra was once a resident of Pell's Gate, but now has left. Some claim to have seen her recently near the Vindasel Ruins.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAClavicusVile_STAGE4_LOG0: I have met the one called Umbra and been offered a choice: stay and fight, or leave in peace.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAClavicusVile_STAGE5_LOG0: I have chosen to leave Umbra in peace. I should return to the Shrine of Clavicus Vile and make my decision known to the Daedra Lord.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAClavicusVile_STAGE6_LOG0: I have retrieved the Umbra Sword.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAClavicusVile_STAGE7_LOG0: Clavicus Vile appreciated my efforts. He has taken the Umbra Sword and rewarded me with the Masque of Clavicus Vile.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAClavicusVile_STAGE8_LOG0: Clavicus Vile was not pleased that I elected not to return to him the Umbra Sword.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAHircine_STAGE0_LOG0: Hircine's followers have told me that, in order to summon the Daedra, I must leave an offering of a wolf or bear pelt at the statue.  [You must be level 2 to begin this quest.]
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAHircine_STAGE1_LOG0: After I made the required offering, Hircine was summoned, and spoke to me. He told me that he desires the horn of a unicorn. I may be able to find a unicorn in the Harcane Grove.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAHircine_STAGE2_LOG0: I have killed the unicorn and retrieved its horn. I should return to the shrine of Hircine.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAHircine_STAGE3_LOG0: Hircine was impressed with my skill in hunting the unicorn. He has rewarded me with the Savior's Hide.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMalacath_STAGE0_LOG0: One of Malacath's worshippers has told me that, in order to have the Daedra summoned, I must leave an offering of Troll Fat at the statue. [You must be level 2 to begin this quest.]
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMalacath_STAGE1_LOG0: When I left the offering, Malacath was summoned and spoke to me. He tasked me with traveling to the estate of Lord Drad and freeing his ogre slaves.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMalacath_STAGE2_LOG0: I have spoken with Lord Drad. He believes the ogres to be suitable slaves, as they are mindless beasts, and is pleased with the ogres he uses in Bleak Mine. I appears I will have to free the ogres myself, if I am to complete Malacath's task for me.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMalacath_STAGE3_LOG0: I have spoken with Lord Drad. He believes the ogres to be suitable slaves, as it does not violate Imperial Law. I fear I've angered him, though, and he will no longer speak with me. Perhaps his wife will be more forthcoming.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMalacath_STAGE4_LOG0: Lady Drad has told me that the ogres are located in Bleak Mine, not far from here. I will have to go free them to appease Malacath.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMalacath_STAGE5_LOG0: I have entered Bleak Mine. Now I must free Malacath's ogres.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMalacath_STAGE6_LOG2: I freed one group of ogres. I must check to see if there are more to be freed.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMalacath_STAGE7_LOG2: I freed one group of ogres. I must check to see if there are more to be freed.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMalacath_STAGE8_LOG0: I have freed all the ogres in the Bleak Mine. I should return to the Shrine of Malacath.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMalacath_STAGE9_LOG0: Malacath was satisfied with my efforts to free the ogres. He has rewarded me with Volendrung.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMalacath_STAGE10_LOG0: Malacath wanted his ogres freed... not killed. Now there's no chance of earning the reward he promised.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMephala_STAGE1_LOG0: Mephala's followers have told me that, in order to summon the Daedra, I must first leave an offering of nightshade at her statue between midnight and dawn. [You must be level 2 to begin this quest.]
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMephala_STAGE2_LOG0: Mephala has told me about the settlement of Bleaker's Way. She would like me to kill the leaders of the two families, and leave evidence that the murders were done by the other family. I am to allow no one to see me commit the crimes.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMephala_STAGE3_LOG0: I have learned Nivan Dalvilu is the head of the Dalvilu family, and Hrol Ulfgar the leader of the Nordic clan.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMephala_STAGE4_LOG0: I have found a Dalvilu ceremonial dagger. This might be useful to leave as evidence.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMephala_STAGE5_LOG0: I have found the Ulfgar family ring. This might be useful to leave as evidence.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMephala_STAGE6_LOG0: I have killed Nivan Dalvilu. I should leave something in his corpse to implicate the Ulfgar clan.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMephala_STAGE7_LOG0: I have left the Ulfgar family ring in the corpse of Nivan Dalvilu.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMephala_STAGE8_LOG0: I have killed Hrol Ulfgar. I should leave something in his corpse to implicate the Dalvilu clan.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMephala_STAGE9_LOG0: I have left the Dalvilu dagger in the corpse of Hrol Ulfgar.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMephala_STAGE10_LOG0: I've placed evidence on both bodies of the family leaders. Now I must tell someone I saw them attack each other.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMephala_STAGE11_LOG0: I reported the 'attacks' on the family leaders. Soon the families will be at each other's throats. Now I must return to the shrine of Mephala.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMephala_STAGE12_LOG0: I've been detected committing a crime in Bleaker's Way. I will not be able to continue my task for Mephala.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMephala_STAGE13_LOG0: Mephala was pleased with the strife I have caused. She has rewarded me with the Ebony Blade.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMephala_STAGE14_LOG0: Mephala was disappointed in my failure.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMephala_STAGE15_LOG0: Something I did in Bleaker's Way apparently spoiled Mephala's schemes, so she refused my offering.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMephala_STAGE16_LOG0: By killing everyone in Bleaker's Way, I've spoiled Mephala's scheme, and spoiled any chance of gaining her favor.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMeridia_STAGE0_LOG0: One of Meridia's followers has told me that the Daedra can be summoned if something from the corpse of an undead creature is left as an offering at the statue. [You must be level 2 to begin this quest.]
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMeridia_STAGE1_LOG0: After I made the correct offering, Meridia appeared and spoke to me. The Daedra told me of a cult of necromancers in the Howling Cave who have been stealing from graves in order to raise an undead army. Meridia wants the necromancers killed. I can reach them from a secret door in the back of the cave.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMeridia_STAGE2_LOG0: I have killed the necromancers in Howling Cave. I should return to the Shrine of Meridia.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMeridia_STAGE3_LOG0: Meridia was gratified that I had performed the task assigned to me. As a reward, I have been given the Ring of Khajiiti.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANamira_STAGE0_LOG0: I have learned from the followers of Namira, that they will only summon the Daedra for someone repulsive. If I am to get their help, I will need to make myself less attractive to others. [You must be level 2 to begin this quest.]
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANamira_STAGE1_LOG0: The followers of Namira have decided that I am repulsive enough to summon the Daedra. I may now approach the statue.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANamira_STAGE2_LOG0: When summoned, Namira spoke to me about the Forgotten, a group of fanatical worshippers that lives in the darkness of Anga. Some priests of Arkay are planning to bring light to Anga and "save" the Forgotten Ones. I am to use the spell that Namira has given me on the priests, and let the Forgotten Ones finish them off.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANamira_STAGE3_LOG0: All of the priests of Arkay are dead. I should return to the shrine.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANamira_STAGE4_LOG0: Namira appreciated my efforts. I have been rewarded with the Ring of Namira.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANamira_STAGE5_LOG0: I have killed one of the priests of Arkay, though I was to allow Namira's Forgotten to do it. I have failed in the task set out by Namira.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANamira_STAGE6_LOG0: I have killed one of Namira's Forgotten Ones. I have failed my quest for the Daedric Prince.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANocturnal_STAGE0_LOG0: I have found the Shrine to Nocturnal and spoken with her followers. They have agreed to allow me to speak to the Daedra. I may approach the statue. [You must be level 2 to begin this quest.]
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANocturnal_STAGE1_LOG0: When summoned, Nocturnal spoke to me of the Eye of Nocturnal, which has been stolen. The Daedra believes the thieves to be in Leyawiin. I am to go there, find the thieves, and return the Eye to the shrine.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANocturnal_STAGE2_LOG0: I have been told that Weebam-Na and Bejeen were recently heard talking about a valuable jewel they had found, and how it would make them wealthy.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANocturnal_STAGE3_LOG0: Weebam-Na is dead, so I can't learn where he has hidden the Eye of Nocturnal... except by searching every dark, wet place in the world.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANocturnal_STAGE4_LOG0: Bejeen is dead, so I can't learn where she has hidden the Eye of Nocturnal... except by searching every dark, wet place in the world.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANocturnal_STAGE5_LOG0: Weebam-Na denies knowing anything about the Eye of Nocturnal, and tells me Bejeen knows nothing, either. Perhaps I should try to listen in on their conversations, and see if they offer any clues.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANocturnal_STAGE6_LOG0: Bejeen denies knowing anything about the Eye of Nocturnal, and tells me Weebam-Na knows nothing, either. Perhaps I should try to listen in on their conversations, and see if they offer any clues.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANocturnal_STAGE7_LOG0: I overheard Bejeen and Weebam-Na talking, but they stopped when they realized I was there. I must eavesdrop on them without being detected.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANocturnal_STAGE8_LOG0: I overheard Bejeen and Weebam-Na speaking of Tidewater Cave, and the treasure they've hidden there. I should investigate the cave.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANocturnal_STAGE12_LOG0: Weebam-Na suspects I have been eavesdropping on his conversations. I must get to Tidewater Cave and look for the Eye of Nocturnal.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANocturnal_STAGE13_LOG0: I've found the Eye of Nocturnal. I should return to the Shrine.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DANocturnal_STAGE14_LOG0: Nocturnal was pleased that I have returned the Eye of Nocturnal. I have been rewarded with the Skeleton Key.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAPeryite_STAGE0_LOG0: I have found the Shrine to Peryite, but something is wrong with his worshippers. They seem frozen in time, mindless. None move or speak. [You must be level 2 to begin this quest.]
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAPeryite_STAGE1_LOG0: Peryite has told me that five of his followers, in an attempt to get closer to Him, have transported their souls to a plane of Oblivion. The Daedra would like me to travel there and carry their souls back to this world. I should approach the statue again when I am prepared to go.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAPeryite_STAGE2_LOG0: I have captured the soul of Kewan.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAPeryite_STAGE3_LOG0: I have captured the soul of Maren the Seal.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAPeryite_STAGE4_LOG0: I have captured the soul of Ilvel Romayn.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAPeryite_STAGE5_LOG0: I have captured the soul of Mirie.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAPeryite_STAGE6_LOG0: I have captured the soul of Er-Teeus.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAPeryite_STAGE7_LOG0: I have found all of the souls of Peryite's followers lost in Oblivion. Peryite was to open a gate for me when they were found. I should head back to where I entered this realm.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAPeryite_STAGE8_LOG0: I have returned to the mortal realm, and the souls of Peryite's followers have gone back to their bodies. I should speak with the Daedra again.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAPeryite_STAGE9_LOG0: Peryite was grateful for my help. He has given me Spell Breaker as a reward.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DASanguine_STAGE0_LOG0: The followers of Sanguine have told me that, in order to summon the Daedra, I will need to leave an offering of Cyrodilic Brandy at the statue. [You must be level 2 to begin this quest.]
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DASanguine_STAGE1_LOG0: After I provided the offering to Sanguine, the Daedra Prince spoke to me. Sanguine believes that the Countess of Leyawiin is too stuffy, and wants me to do something about it. She will be hosting a dinner party tomorrow, and I am to sneak into the castle undetected and cast the spell of Stark Reality on her.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DASanguine_STAGE2_LOG0: I have convinced the guard to let me into the Countess Caro's dinner party.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DASanguine_STAGE3_LOG0: I have succeeded in casting the Stark Reality spell on the Countess of Leyawiin and her company. I should return to the Shrine of Sanguine.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DASanguine_STAGE4_LOG0: I was detected while attempting to cast the spell of Stark Reality on the Countess of Leyawiin and her guests. I should return to the Shrine of Sanguine.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DASanguine_STAGE5_LOG0: Sanguine was pleased with my efforts. He has given me the Sanguine Rose as a reward. I may retrieve my belongings in the nearby chest.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DASanguine_STAGE6_LOG0: I failed to affect all the guests with the Stark Reality spell. The spell has worn off, and my items have been returned to me, but I have failed my quest for Sanguine.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAVaermina_STAGE0_LOG0: Vaermina's followers have told me that an offering of a black soul gem is needed in order to summon the Daedra. [You must be level 2 to begin this quest.]
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAVaermina_STAGE1_LOG0: After I left an offering of a black soul gem, Vaermina's followers summoned the Daedra, who spoke to me. Vaermina told me of an Orb that had been stolen by the wizard Arkved. I am to travel to his tower, retrieve the Orb, and bring it back to Vaermina.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAVaermina_STAGE2_LOG0: I have retrieved the Orb of Vaermina. I should now return it to the Shrine.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAVaermina_STAGE3_LOG0: Vaermina was pleased that I returned the Orb of Vaermina. I have been rewarded with the Skull of Corruption.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE1_LOG0: I should speak to Jauffre about how to recover the Amulet from the enemy.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE2_LOG0: Jauffre wants me to contact Baurus at Luther Broad's Boarding House in the Imperial City. The enemy has the Amulet of Kings, and Baurus may have the leads we need to get it back.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE3_LOG0: I must follow the man who is watching Baurus and me.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE6_LOG0: The enemy agent who attacked Baurus is dead. I should find out what Baurus has learned about our enemies.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE7_LOG0: Baurus wants me to search the dead agent's body.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE8_LOG0: I have found a strange book about the Mythic Dawn cult. I should show this to Baurus.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE9_LOG0: Baurus has learned that the enemy is a secret cult of Mehrunes Dagon worshippers known as the Mythic Dawn. He will continue to track down agents in the Imperial City. I should talk to Tar-Meena at the Arcane University, an expert in daedric cults. She may be able to tell me more about how to find the Mythic Dawn.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE10_LOG0: Tar-Meena told me that Mankar Camoran is the leader of the Mythic Dawn. Tar-Meena believes that the location of their secret shrine is hidden within Mankar Camoran's books about the Mysterium Xarxes. If I want to locate the Mythic Dawn, I must find and decipher the clues in all four volumes.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE10_LOG1: Tar-Meena gave me the second of Mankar Camoran's books about the Mysterium Xarxes, but said that she has never seen Mankar Camoran's third and fourth books on the Mysterium Xarxes. She suggested that I speak to Phintias, the prioprietor of the First Edition bookstore in the Market District.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE12_LOG0: I now have Volume 3 of the four books I need. I should search Gwinas's body for clues to what he wanted with it.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE12_LOG1: I now have Volume 3 of the four books I need. I now need to meet with the Sponsor to get Volume 4, but I should find Baurus first.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE12_LOG2: I now have Volume 3 of the four books I need, but I should find out why the bookseller had it.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE12_LOG3: I now have Volume 3 of the four books I need. I should wait for Gwinas to come to the store, and see why he wanted it.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE12_LOG4: I now have Volume 3 of the four books I need. I still need to find out what Gwinas wanted with it.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE13_LOG0: Phintias, the bookseller at First Edition, has told me that he has a copy of Volume 3 of Mankar Camoran's "Commentaries on the Mysterium Xarxes" locked away. He is holding it for a client named Gwinas who has already paid for it. I need to get that book, and find out why Gwinas wanted it.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE13_LOG1: Phintias told me he was holding Volume 3 of Mankar Camoran's "Commentaries on the Mysterium Xarxes" for a client named Gwinas, who has already paid for it. I should wait for Gwinas to come to the store, and see why he wanted it.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE14_LOG0: Phintias told me that Gwinas is on his way to pick up his copy of Volume 3. I should wait for him to arrive and find out what Gwinas knows.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE15_LOG0: Gwinas has picked up his copy of Volume 3. I need to get that book from Gwinas.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE16_LOG0: I found out that Gwinas is staying at the Tiber Septim Hotel in the Talos Plaza District. I need to find Gwinas and get Volume 3 of Camoran's "Commentaries" from him.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE17_LOG0: I found out that Gwinas is staying at the Tiber Septim Hotel in the Talos Plaza District. I need to find out what Gwinas wanted with Volume 3 of Camoran's "Commentaries".
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE18_LOG0: Gwinas told me that Volume 4, the last book I need, can only be given to you by a member of the Mythic Dawn. He had set up a meeting with one, whom he called "The Sponsor". He was to go alone and sit at the table in the Sunken Sewer beneath the Imperial City. I will go and take that meeting instead, but should find Baurus first.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE19_LOG0: I took a note from Gwinas, from someone in the Mythic Dawn calling themselves "The Sponsor". The note explains that Volume 4, one of the books I need, can only be given to you by a member of the Mythic Dawn. He was to go alone and sit at the table in the Sunken Sewer beneath the Imperial City, where The Sponsor would meet him. I will go and take that meeting instead, but must get Volume 3 first.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE19_LOG1: I took a note from Gwinas, from someone in the Mythic Dawn calling themselves "The Sponsor". The note explains that Volume 4, the last book I need, can only be given to you by a member of the Mythic Dawn. He was to go alone and sit at the table in the Sunken Sewer beneath the Imperial City, where The Sponsor would meet him. I will go and take that meeting instead, but should find Baurus first.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE21_LOG0: I must follow Baurus to the Sunken Sewer beneath the Elven Gardens District and obtain Volume 4 of Mankar Camoran's "Commentaries on the Mysterium Xarxes". One of us will need to meet "The Sponsor" alone to get the book.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE23_LOG0: I've decided to be the one to take the book from The Sponsor and Baurus will provide backup. No matter what happens, I must get that book.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE24_LOG0: I've decided Baurus will be the one to take the book from The Sponsor and I will provide backup. No matter what happens, we must get that book.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE27_LOG1: The Sponsor has realized that I did not come alone. I must get the fourth book from him!
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE27_LOG2: The Sponsor has realized that Baurus did not come alone. I must get the fourth book from him!
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE28_LOG0: The Sponsor wants me dead! I need to get the fourth book of Mankar Camoran's "Commentaries" from him no matter what.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE28_LOG1: The Sponsor wants Baurus dead! I need to get the fourth book of Mankar Camoran's "Commentaries" from him no matter what.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE30_LOG0: I have the fourth and last volume of Mankar Camoran's writings on the Mysterium Xarxes. I can now piece together the clues within them to find the Mythic Dawn's secret shrine. Maybe Tar-Meena can help.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE31_LOG0: Tar-Meena says that Mankar Camoran hid a message within his writings, so only the "enlightened ones" could join him and the Mythic Dawn. I should study these books some more.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE32_LOG0: Tar-Meena thinks using the first word of each paragraph in the books may reveal a message. I need to keep looking and see where this leads.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MQ05_STAGE33_LOG0: Tar-Meena has discovered the message in the books: "Green Emperor Way Where Tower Touches Midday Sun". I should go to Green Emperor Way by the Imperial Tower and see what can be found.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE0_LOG0: Armand told me that he won't have any commission jobs for me until I sell more stolen property to Ongar the fence. I'll need about 100 gold worth of stolen goods before he will trust me sufficiently.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE1_LOG0: I've reached my goal. I have fenced over 100 gold worth of goods. I should go see Armand sometime. He might have something for me.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE2_LOG0: Armand told me that he won't have another commission job for me until I sell more stolen property to Ongar the fence. I'll need about 500 gold worth of stolen goods before he will trust me sufficiently.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE3_LOG0: I've reached my goal. I have fenced over 500 gold worth of goods. I should go see Armand sometime. He might have something for me.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE4_LOG0: S'krivva told me that she won't have another commission job for me until I sell more stolen property to Ongar or Dar Jee. I'll need about 1000 gold worth of stolen goods before she will trust me sufficiently.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE5_LOG0: I've reached my goal. I have fenced over 1000 gold worth of goods. I should go see S'krivva sometime. She might have something for me.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE6_LOG0: S'krivva told me that she won't have any commission jobs for me until I sell more stolen property to Ongar or Dar Jee. I'll need about 2000 gold worth of stolen goods before she will trust me sufficiently.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE7_LOG0: I've reached my goal. I have fenced over 2000 gold worth of goods. I should go see S'krivva sometime. She might have something for me.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE8_LOG0: S'krivva told me that she won't have any commission jobs for me until I sell more stolen property to a fence. I'll need about 3000 gold worth of stolen goods before she will trust me sufficiently. I should check out the new fence, Luciana Galena.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE9_LOG0: I've reached my goal. I have fenced over 3000 gold worth of goods. I should go see S'krivva sometime. She might have something for me.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE10_LOG0: S'krivva told me that she won't have another commission job for me until I sell more stolen property to the guild fences, like Luciana Galena. I'll need about 4000 gold worth of stolen goods before she will trust me sufficiently.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE11_LOG0: I've reached my goal. I have fenced over 4000 gold worth of goods. I should go see S'krivva sometime. She might have something for me.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE12_LOG0: S'krivva told me that she has no more commission jobs for me. However, the Gray Fox may be contacting me soon. Until then I sell more stolen property to the guild fences. I'll need at least 5000 gold worth of stolen goods to get his attention.. I should see what kinds of prices I can get from the new fence, Orrin.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE13_LOG0: I've reached my goal. I have fenced over 5000 gold worth of goods. When I'm in the cities, I'll have to keep an eye out for messengers from the Gray Fox. He could call for me at any time. I should wait in the Imperial City if I want them to find me quickly.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE14_LOG0: The Gray Fox may be contacting me soon. Until then I sell more stolen property to the guild fences, like Orrin. I'll need at least 7500 gold worth of stolen goods to get his attention..
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE15_LOG0: I've reached my goal. I have fenced over 7500 gold worth of goods. When I'm in the cities, I'll have to keep an eye out for another messenger from the Gray Fox. He could call for me at any time. I should wait in the Imperial City if I want them to find me quickly.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE16_LOG0: The Gray Fox may be contacting me soon. Until then I sell more stolen property to the guild fences. I'll need at least 10000 gold worth of stolen goods to get his attention.. Now I can use the best fence the guild has, Fathis Ules.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE17_LOG0: I've reached my goal. I have fenced over 10000 gold worth of goods. When I'm in the cities, I'll have to keep an eye out for another messenger from the Gray Fox. He could call for me at any time. I should wait in the Imperial City if I want them to find me quickly.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE18_LOG0: The Gray Fox may be contacting me soon. Until then I sell more stolen property to the guild fences, such as Fathis Ules. I'll need at least 15000 gold worth of stolen goods to get his attention..
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TGStolenGoods_STAGE19_LOG0: I've reached my goal. I have fenced over 15000 gold worth of goods. When I'm in the cities, I'll have to keep an eye out for another messenger from the Gray Fox. He could call for me at any time. I should wait in the Imperial City if I want them to find me quickly.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MG04Restore_STAGE0_LOG0: I need to speak with Arielle Jurard at the Brina Cross Inn, north of Anvil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MG04Restore_STAGE1_LOG0: I need to rent a bed for the night at the Brina Cross Inn, while posing as a traveling merchant.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MG04Restore_STAGE2_LOG0: I have rented a bed at the Brina Cross Inn. I need to wait in my room for Arielle Jurard, and then spend the night sleeping in the Inn.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MG04Restore_STAGE3_LOG0: Arielle Jurard has instructed me to sleep at the Brina Cross Inn, then travel along the Gold Road towards Kvatch.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MG04Restore_STAGE4_LOG0: I've spent the night at the Brina Cross Inn. I need to follow the Gold Road towards Kvatch, and be wary of anyone along the road.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MG04Restore_STAGE5_LOG0: I need to talk to Carahil in Anvil and inform her that Caminalda has been defeated.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MG04Restore_STAGE6_LOG0: I have completed my task for Carahil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_FGD05Oreyn_STAGE0_LOG0: Oreyn would like to speak more about duties, once I am promoted to the next rank.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_FGD05Oreyn_STAGE1_LOG0: I went to Modryn Oreyn to ask about duties, but he told me he had no "official" duties. However, he asked me to meet him at his house later tonight.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_FGD05Oreyn_STAGE2_LOG0: I met Modryn Oreyn at his house, as he requested. He told me a story about Azani Blackheart, Vitellus Donton's death, and the Blackwood Company's current involvement. He would like me to go with him now to find out what really happened.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_FGD05Oreyn_STAGE3_LOG0: I've told Oreyn that I will not accompany him in investigating how the Blackwood Company fulfilled the Azani Blackheart contract.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_FGD05Oreyn_STAGE4_LOG0: I am to meet Oreyn at the Fighters Guild in Leyawiin. From there, we will travel to Arpenia to look for Azani Blackheart.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_FGD05Oreyn_STAGE5_LOG0: We've searched the ruins of Arpenia, and the place is deserted. There are no corpses, no signs of battle. Oreyn believes that the entire story was made up by the Blackwood Company, that perhaps they paid off Azani Blackheart to move his base of operations. Oreyn suggests we search nearby Ayleid ruins, and knows of one to the northeast.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_FGD05Oreyn_STAGE6_LOG0: Oreyn and I have found and defeated Azani Blackheart.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_FGD05Oreyn_STAGE7_LOG0: Oreyn wishes to make sure people know who truly defeated the great warrior, and recognize the lies of the Blackwood Company. Oreyn would like me to give him Azani Blackheart's Ring as proof of our accomplishments here today.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_FGD05Oreyn_STAGE8_LOG0: I have given the ring to Oreyn. This will allow him to prove that it was the Fighters Guild who finally defeated Azani Blackheart.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS27_STAGE2_LOG0: Umbacano has hired me to obtain an Ayleid Crown he wants from Herminia Cinna, a rival collector. He paid me in advance; whatever I do not use to purchase the crown I can keep as my fee.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS27_STAGE3_LOG0: Umbacano hired me to retrieve the Crown of the Ayleids from Herminia Cinna, a rival collector. By chance, I had already stolen the crown, and was able to deliver it immediately, to Umbacano's great surprise.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS27_STAGE4_LOG0: Herminia Cinna refused to sell me the Crown of Nenalata that Umbacano wants. She hinted that it can be used to access potent magical powers that would dangerous in the wrong hands.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS27_STAGE4_LOG1: Herminia Cinna gave me the key to the royal burial vault of Lindai, which she believes holds a similar Ayleid Crown. She suggested that Umbacano would not know the difference between the Crown of Lindai and the Crown of Nenalata, since he has never seen either one.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS27_STAGE4_LOG2: I need to bring Umbacano one of the Ayleid Crowns, either by stealing the Crown of Nenalata from Herminia Cinna, or by recovering the Crown of Lindai as Herminia Cinna suggested.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS27_STAGE5_LOG0: I have retrieved the Ayleid Crown from Herminia Cinna's house. Now to deliver it to Umbacano.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS27_STAGE6_LOG0: I have found the Ayleid Crown of Lindai, just as Herminia Cinna said. Now to return to Umbacano to complete my task.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS27_STAGE7_LOG0: I gave Umbacano the Ayleid Crown of Nenalata, as he requested.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS27_STAGE8_LOG0: I gave Umbacano the Ayleid Crown of Lindai, as Herminia Cinna suggested. He did not appear to be aware that it was not the Ayleid Crown that he had asked me to retrieve.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS27_STAGE9_LOG0: Umbacano has asked me to meet him at the Ayleid ruin of Nenalata. He wants me to help him reach the throne room so that he can complete his research on the last king of the Ayleids.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS27_STAGE10_LOG0: I met Umbacano outside the ruins of Nenalata. I need to guide him safely to the throne room.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS27_STAGE12_LOG0: Umbacano is dead. The only thing left to do is try to escape this death trap!
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS27_STAGE15_LOG0: Umbacano died during the expedition to Nenalata. I failed in my duty to protect him. Now I will never know what secrets are hidden in the Throne Room of Nenalata.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS27_STAGE16_LOG0: Umbacano was transformed into an evil Ayleid King in the throne room of Nenalata. I must escape from this death trap!
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS27_STAGE17_LOG0: Umbacano was horribly destroyed in Nenalata when he declared himself King of the Ayleids using the crown of the rival city of Lindai. Now to escape the wrath of the undead denizens of Nenalata!
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS26_STAGE2_LOG0: Apparently there is a corrupt Imperial Watchman in the city. Luronk gro-Glurzog in the Temple district was complaining about him. Luronk ran afoul of him at Jensine's Merchandise in the market district. Luronk didn't have a name, but maybe Jensine does.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS26_STAGE3_LOG0: Apparently there is a corrupt Imperial Watchman in the city. Ruslan in the Temple district was complaining about him. Ruslan ran afoul of him at Jensine's Merchandise in the market district. Ruslan didn't have a name, but maybe Jensine does.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS26_STAGE4_LOG0: I spoke to Jensine in the Imperial market district about the corrupt watchman. She told me the same story as Ruslan and Luronk. The Imperial Watch Captain Audens Avidius is extorting money from the shopkeepers. They are all too afraid of him to do anything about it. I need to find someone with the authority to stop him. She says only another watch captain would be able to arrest him.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS26_STAGE5_LOG0: The guards are no help. Any complaints about guard activities are to be taken to the guard captain. Of course Audens Avidius is one of the guard captains, so that won't work. I could try talking to one of the other Watch Captains.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS26_STAGE6_LOG0: I tried reporting Audens Avidius to Hieronymus Lex, who is one of the other guard captains. He is busy chasing after the Gray Fox. He said I should try the third guard captain, Itius Hayn.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS26_STAGE7_LOG0: I tried reporting Audens Avidius to Servatius Quintilius, who is one of the other guard captains. He is new to the job and won't act against one of the other guard captains. He said I should try the third guard captain, Itius Hayn.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS26_STAGE8_LOG0: Watch Captain Itius Hayn is willing to arrest Audens Avidius if I can produce two witnesses that will publicly accuse him. The only victims I know are Ruslan, Luronk gro-Glurzog, and Jensine. I'll need to talk to them.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS26_STAGE9_LOG0: Jensine refuses to testify against Audens Avidius. I don't think I can persuade her. I'll have to try the other two, Luronk and Ruslan.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS26_STAGE10_LOG0: Ruslan has agreed to testify against Audens Avidius in the morning. Now I just have to get Luronk gro-Glurzog to testify as well.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS26_STAGE12_LOG0: Luronk gro-Glurzog has agreed to testify against Audens Avidius in the morning. Now I just have to get Ruslan to testify as well.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS26_STAGE14_LOG0: I've convinced both Ruslan and Luronk to testify against Audens Avidius. I hope Itius Hayn is true to his word and arrests his fellow Watch Captain. Otherwise those two are going to be in a lot of trouble. I could keep an eye on those two and make sure they go to see Itius first thing in the morning.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS26_STAGE17_LOG0: Audens Avidius has been arrested. That should make Jensine, Ruslan, and Luronk happy. Hopefully it will serve as a lesson for any other guard that is thinking about shaking down innocent citizens.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS26_STAGE20_LOG0: Audens Avidius is dead. He was that crooked guard in the Imperial City that had threatened Luronk and Ruslan. He must have escaped from prison. He kept raving about how he would get me for putting him in jail.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS26_STAGE21_LOG0: Audens Avidius attacked me. He was that crooked guard in the Imperial City that had threatened Luronk and Ruslan. He must have escaped from prison. Sadly, I was forced to kill him. He kept raving about how he would get me for putting him in jail.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TG07Lex_STAGE1_LOG0: A beggar told me I could find S'krivva at the Lonely Suitor Lodge.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TG07Lex_STAGE2_LOG0: I have learned that Dairihill's office is in the castle's private quarters, which is off limits. I should look for an indirect way to get in there.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TG07Lex_STAGE4_LOG0: S'krivva tells me that the Gray Fox is tired of Hieronymus Lex causing problems. He wants Lex removed once and for all. Countess Umbranox of Anvil is in need of a Captain of the Guard. She sent away for a list of recommendations from the Imperial Watch guard. I must create a forgery that recommends Lex and deliver it to her personally. The Gray Fox has a special interest in Countess Umbranox and has chosen Lex to be her protector. Very curious.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TG07Lex_STAGE4_LOG1: First, I steal the letter from steward Dairihill's desk in Castle Anvil. Then get a forgery made. I have to find and pay for my own forger. Then seal it with the Imperial Watch seal from the legion commander's desk in the Imperial City. Dairihill cannot be trusted to deliver the letter. I must personally deliver the forged letter to Countess Umbranox of Anvil. I will be paid 500 gold, plus the cost of the forgery.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TG07Lex_STAGE5_LOG0: A beggar told me that there may be secret ways in the castle. I am supposed to find the blacksmith. I'm not really sure why.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TG07Lex_STAGE6_LOG0: Orrin has shown me a secret passage within the walls of Castle Anvil. It should let me get closer to Dairihill's office without being seen by the castle guards.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TG07Lex_STAGE7_LOG0: My sources tell me that there is a master forger in Anvil. He is known only as The Stranger. He lives in a house by the Mages Guild.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TG07Lex_STAGE8_LOG0: I have the letter. Now I need to find a forger.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TG07Lex_STAGE9_LOG0: I delivered the letter to The Stranger. He said to return in a day and he would have the new letter ready. It's going to cost 500 gold coins.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TG07Lex_STAGE10_LOG0: The Stranger has forged a new letter for me. Now I have to go to the Imperial Legion compound in the Imperial City. I have to seal the letter using the official seal of the Legion Commander.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TG07Lex_STAGE11_LOG0: I've sealed the letter with the Legion Commander's seal. Now I must deliver it to Countess Umbranox personally. I will take on the role of courier.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TG07Lex_STAGE14_LOG0: Countess Umbranox has given me her response. Now I need to deliver it to Hieronymus Lex. The look on his face should be priceless.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TG07Lex_STAGE15_LOG0: Hieronymus Lex was shocked to learn he had been reassigned. Now the Thieves Guild will be safe from his persecution. All I have to do is get back to S'krivva to collect my reward.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TG07Lex_STAGE16_LOG0: S'krivva thanked me for my help in this delicate matter. Hieronymus Lex will no longer be a threat to the Thieves Guild or the Gray Fox.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS02_STAGE0_LOG0: I've overheard a rumor that Velwyn Benirus wants to sell a manor here in Anvil, and that he's selling it cheap. I should go find him and see what he has to offer.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS02_STAGE1_LOG0: I've met Velwyn Benirus. He has a manor house for sale here in Anvil. I should question him further to see how much he wants for it.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS02_STAGE2_LOG0: I've purchased the manor from Velwyn. He gave me a key and the deed of ownership. I can now move in there at any time.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS02_STAGE3_LOG0: I've arrived at my new house in Anvil, Benirus Manor. Looks like the place may need some work, but it was a great deal I just couldn't pass up. I can now make myself at home.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS02_STAGE4_LOG0: Benirus Manor is haunted! I was awakened by ghosts that attacked me as I slept. As I awoke, I thought I heard a crash from downstairs. I should search the house and investigate.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS02_STAGE5_LOG0: I've found a skeletal hand next to a page from a diary. The diary makes mention of a secret room in the house that only a true-blooded Benirus can open. I am going to need to talk to Velwyn about this. I may have to ask around town to find out where he's gone.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS02_STAGE6_LOG0: I've discovered that Velwyn has left town and made his way to the Imperial City. If I am to find out why Benirus Manor is haunted, I need to find him there.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS02_STAGE7_LOG0: I've been told that Velwyn stays in the King and Queen Tavern here in the Imperial City. I should go there to find him.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS02_STAGE8_LOG0: I've found Velwyn at The King and Queen Tavern in the Imperial City. I should speak to him further about the manor and the strange goings on within.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS02_STAGE9_LOG0: After questioning Velwyn, I've learned that his grandfather dabbled in necromancy and was killed when the townspeople of Anvil found out. Strangely, the body was never recovered. Since then, the house has been cursed. I need to convince Velwyn to come back with me to Anvil and lift the curse.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS02_STAGE10_LOG0: Velwyn agreed to meet me back in Anvil at The Count's Arms. I should return there as soon as possible.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS02_STAGE11_LOG0: I've met Velwyn at The Count's Arms Tavern. He's agreed to escort me to the house and open the secret room.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS02_STAGE12_LOG0: Velwyn and I have arrived in Benirus Manor. I must proceed cautiously to the secret room's entrance and prevent any harm from befalling us if the curse is to ever be lifted from this place.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS02_STAGE13_LOG0: Velwyn and I have arrived at the secret door.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS02_STAGE14_LOG0: Velwyn successfully opened the door to the secret room, which has stopped the ghosts from attacking. He then ran off, leaving me to clean up the mess. It's up to me, and me alone, to investigate what's beyond the mysterious portal.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS02_STAGE15_LOG0: After touching the altar, I heard the voice of Lorgren Benirus. He said he wished to atone for his past and make peace with the Nines. All he wished was to be whole again. This must be the way to lift the curse from Benirus Manor. I should touch his skeleton while I carry the bony hand and put an end to this madness.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS02_STAGE16_LOG0: Completing the skeleton was a ruse and Lorgren rose from the dead! I had no choice but to destroy him. With his destruction, the curse of Benirus Manor was lifted. I should return to The Count's Arms and speak to Velwyn.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_MS02_STAGE17_LOG0: I have met Velwyn back at The Count's Arms. He congratulated me and then told me he would be off again to live in the Imperial City. Now I can truly call Benirus Manor my home.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseLeyawiin_STAGE0_LOG0: I just bought a house in Leyawiin. It's right in the middle of town.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseLeyawiin_STAGE1_LOG0: Now that I've arrived at my new house in Leyawiin, I may want to head over to Gundalas at Best Goods and Guarantees to secure some furnishings for it.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseLeyawiin_STAGE2_LOG0: I've now bought everything Gundalas has for my house in Leyawiin.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseBravil_STAGE0_LOG0: I just bought a house in Bravil on the east side of the canal.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseBravil_STAGE1_LOG0: Now that I've arrived at my new house in Bravil, I may want to head over to Nilawen at The Fair Deal to secure some furnishings for it.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseBravil_STAGE2_LOG0: I've now bought everything Nilawen has for my house in Bravil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseBruma_STAGE0_LOG0: I just bought a house in Bruma. It's right next to the main gate.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseBruma_STAGE1_LOG0: Now that I've arrived at my new house in Bruma, I may want to head over to Suurootan at Novaroma to secure some furnishings for it.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseBruma_STAGE2_LOG0: I've now bought everything Suurootan has for my house in Bruma.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseChorrol_STAGE0_LOG0: I just bought a manor in Chorrol. It's on the main plaza and back up against the chapel.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseChorrol_STAGE1_LOG0: Now that I've arrived at my new manor in Chorrol, I may want to head over to Seed-Neeus at Northern Goods and Trade to secure some furnishings for it.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseChorrol_STAGE2_LOG0: I've now bought everything Seed-Neeus has for my manor in Chorrol.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseCheydinhal_STAGE0_LOG0: I just bought a house in Cheydinhal. It's right next to the town statue.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseCheydinhal_STAGE1_LOG0: Now that I've arrived at my new house in Cheydinhal, I may want to head over to Borba gra-Uzgash at Borba's Goods and Stores to secure some furnishings for it.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseCheydinhal_STAGE2_LOG0: I've now bought everything Borba gra-Uzgash has for my house in Cheydinhal.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseSkingrad_STAGE0_LOG0: I was told that I need to speak with Shum gro-Yarug to buy a manor in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseSkingrad_STAGE1_LOG0: I just bought a manor in Skingrad. It's just inside the main gate, next to the bridge that leads to the Chapel.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseSkingrad_STAGE2_LOG0: Now that I've arrived at my new manor in Skingrad, I may want to head over to Gunder at Colovian Traders to secure some furnishings for it.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_HouseSkingrad_STAGE3_LOG0: I've now bought everything Gunder has for my manor in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TrainingDestruction_STAGE0_LOG0: I must seek out Bralsa Andaren if I wish to learn more about Destruction magic.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TrainingDestruction_STAGE1_LOG0: I must bring 20 bear pelts to Bralsa Andaren so that she will agree to teach me what she knows about Destruction magic.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TrainingDestruction_STAGE2_LOG0: I have met Bralsa Andaren's demands, and she has agreed to teach me what she knows.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TrainingDestruction_STAGE3_LOG0: Bralsa Andaren is dead. I can no longer be taught more about Destruction.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TrainingAcrobatics_STAGE0_LOG0: For further training in Acrobatics, I need to search the mountains in the northeast of Cyrodiil for Aerin's Camp.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TrainingAcrobatics_STAGE1_LOG0: Torbern has agreed to train me in Acrobatics in Aerin's absence.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TrainingAlteration_STAGE0_LOG0: To learn more about Alteration, I must find Tooth-in-the-Sea, who lives north of Bravil, near Niben Bay.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TrainingAlteration_STAGE1_LOG0: Tooth-in-the-Sea has told me I must prove myself; I need to meet him out in Niben Bay during the day.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TrainingAlteration_STAGE2_LOG0: I must remain with Tooth-in-the-Sea for three consecutive hours if I am to prove myself worthy of his training.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TrainingAlteration_STAGE3_LOG0: Tooth-in-the-Sea has agreed to teach me what he knows about Alteration magic.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_TrainingAlteration_STAGE4_LOG0: Tooth-in-the-Sea is dead, making it impossible to further my training in the skill of Alteration.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE10_LOG0: Molag Bal's follower has told me that, in order to summon the Daedra, I must present an offering of a lion pelt to the statue. [You must be level 2 to begin this quest.]
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE20_LOG0: Molag Bal has spoken to me, and wishes me to help him corrupt Melus Petilius, by forcing him to kill me with the Cursed Mace. I should travel to Brindle Home and see what I can find.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE30_LOG0: I have spoken to a resident of Brindle Home, who tells me that Petilius has lived in a small house outside of town since the death of his wife.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE40_LOG0: I have learned that Petilius visits the grave of his wife every day.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE50_LOG0: Although I tried to goad Petilius into fighting me, he would not. Perhaps I will have to try when he is in a different state of mind.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE60_LOG0: I have goaded Melus Petilius into attacking me with the Cursed Mace. I have been transported back to the Shrine of Molag Bal.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE60_LOG1: I have goaded Melus Petilius into attacking me, but not with the Cursed Mace. I have been transported back to Molag Bal's shrine.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE5_LOG2: I have goaded Melus Petilius into attacking me, but not with the Cursed Mace. I have been transported back to Molag Bal's shrine. The Nine are offended at my transgression against their former Paladin. With this action I have forsaken the possibility of wearing Melus Petilius' armor and wielding  his holy weapons.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE5_LOG3: I have goaded Melus Petilius into attacking me with the Cursed Mace. I have been transported back to the Shrine of Molag Bal. Lets see if the bargain proves useful to me in the end. The Nine are offended at my transgression against their former Paladin. With this action I have forsaken the possibility of wearing Melus Petilius' armor and wielding his holy weapons.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE5_LOG4: I have goaded Melus Petilius into attacking me, but not with the Cursed Mace. I have been transported back to Molag Bal's shrine.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE5_LOG5: I have goaded Melus Petilius into attacking me with the Cursed Mace. I have been transported back to the Shrine of Molag Bal.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE7_LOG0: I allowed Petilius to kill me, but he did not use the Cursed Mace. I have failed in the task given to me by Molag Bal.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE7_LOG1: I allowed Petilius to kill me, but he did not use the Cursed Mace. I have failed in the task given to me by Molag Bal. Also, the Nine will never allow me to retrieve Melus Petilius' weapon and armor.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE7_LOG2: I allowed Petilius to kill me, but he did not use the Cursed Mace. I have failed in the task given to me by Molag Bal.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE8_LOG0: Melus Petilius is dead, so I cannot complete the task given to me by Molag Bal. I doubt that he will now reward me in return.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE8_LOG1: Melus Petilius is dead, so I cannot complete the task given to me by Molag Bal. I doubt that he will now reward me in return. Also, the Nine are offended at my transgression against their former Paladin. With this action I have forsaken the possibility of wearing Melus Petilius' armor and wielding his holy weapons.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE8_LOG2: Melus Petilius died when I released Heaven's Fury, so I cannot complete the task given to me by Molag Bal. The Daedra Lord will not favor me, but I have already gained the trust of the Nine.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE0_LOG0: I have found a signet ring bearing a noble crest on a fallen Knight of the Dragonborne in Cracked Wood Cave. I should return it to Sir Alain Uruk in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE0_LOG1: I have found a signet ring bearing a noble crest on a fallen Knight of the Dragonborne in Cracked Wood Cave. Perhaps I should ask around to see if anybody has heard of this noble order.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE1_LOG0: I have found a signet ring bearing a noble crest on a fallen Elite Knight of the Dragonborne in Cracked Wood Cave. I should return it to Sir Alain Uruk in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE1_LOG1: I have found a signet ring bearing a noble crest on a fallen Elite Knight of the Dragonborne in Fatback Cave. Perhaps I should ask around to see if anybody has heard of this noble order.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE2_LOG0: Glarthir has, in his own imitable fashion, told me that the ring I found bears the crest of the Uruk family. Apparently, Sir Alain Uruk is the leader of the Knights of the Dragonborne, and he can be found here in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE2_LOG1: A beggar has told me that the ring I found bears the crest of the Uruk family. Apparently, Sir Alain Uruk is the leader of the Knights of the Dragonborne, and he can be found here in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE2_LOG2: A beggar has told me that the ring I found bears the crest of the Uruk family. Apparently, Sir Alain Uruk is the leader of the Knights of the Dragonborne, and he can be found in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE2_LOG3: A citizen of Skingrad has told me that the ring I found bears the crest of the Uruk family. Apparently, Sir Alain Uruk is the leader of the Knights of the Dragonborne, and he can be found here in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE2_LOG4: A guard has told me that the ring I found bears the crest of the Uruk family. Apparently, Sir Alain Uruk is the leader of the Knights of the Dragonborne, and he can be found here in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE2_LOG5: Mirisa has told me that she does not recognize the crest on the ring I found, but she does know that the leader of the Knights of the Dragonborne, Sir Alain Uruk, can be found in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE2_LOG6: A Knight of the Dragonborne has told me that the ring I found bears the crest of the Uruk family. Apparently, Sir Alain Uruk is the leader of his order, and he can be found in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE2_LOG7: A guard has told me that the ring I found bears the crest of the Uruk family. Apparently, Sir Alain Uruk is the leader of the Knights of the Dragonborne, and he can be found in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE3_LOG0: I have returned the ring I found on his father's body to Sir Alain Uruk.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE3_LOG1: I have returned the ring I found on the fallen knight to Sir Alain Uruk.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE3_LOG2: I have found Sir Alain Uruk, the leader of the Knights of the Dragonborne, and I have returned the ring I found on his father's body to him.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE3_LOG3: I have found Sir Alain Uruk, the leader of the Knights of the Dragonborne, and I have returned the ring I found on the fallen knight to him.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE4_LOG0: Sir Alain Uruk asked me to help him find his brother, Jim.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE5_LOG0: I have refused to help Sir Alain. If a noble Knight of the Dragonborne can't find his own brother, I wouldn't have much chance.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE6_LOG0: I have agreed to help Sir Alain locate his brother, Jim. Apparently, he was kidnapped by goblins. Seems a strange thing for goblins to do - they usually just kill you.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE8_LOG0: I have found Sir Alain's brother, Jim. It appears that he was a full-fledged member of the White Skin goblin tribe.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE8_LOG1: I have found Sir Alain's brother, Jim. It appears that he is a full-fledged member of the White Skin goblin tribe.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE9_LOG0: When Sir Alain Uruk described his brother to me, I remembered the strange Breton man I encountered living among the White Skin goblin tribe. I fear that was Sir Alain's brother. I had better mention this to him.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE9_LOG1: Sir Alain Uruk's brother, Jim, is dead. I should return to Sir Alain in Skingrad with the sad news.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE10_LOG0: I have told Sir Alain Uruk of the demise of his brother Jim. I'm not sure whether he was upset or angry, but he made me a Knight of the Dragonborne. He asked for some time to grieve for his brother, but I guess he will soon have a noble task for me.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE11_LOG0: The hunter Mirisa suggested that killing the shaman of a goblin tribe would effectively destroy that tribe. Maybe I could put that information to good use...
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE11_LOG1: I remembered something the hunter Mirisa told me, when I helped save Barthel Gernand and his family from the goblins near Cropsford. She told me that killing the shaman of a goblin tribe would effectively destroy that tribe. Maybe I could put that information to good use...
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE12_LOG0: All of the tribal goblin shamans are dead. That should be enough to satisfy Sir Alain Uruk. I should return to Skingrad for my reward.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE12_LOG1: The shaman of the Sharp Tooth goblin tribe is dead. This tribe will no longer be a threat to the citizens of Cyrodiil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE12_LOG2: The shaman of the Three Feathers goblin tribe is dead. This tribe will no longer be a threat to the citizens of Cyrodiil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE12_LOG3: The shaman of the Dust Eater goblin tribe is dead. This tribe will no longer be a threat to the citizens of Cyrodiil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE12_LOG4: The shaman of the Skull Breaker goblin tribe is dead. This tribe will no longer be a threat to the citizens of Cyrodiil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE12_LOG5: The shaman of the Rock Biter goblin tribe is dead. This tribe will no longer be a threat to the citizens of Cyrodiil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE12_LOG6: The shaman of the Bloody Hand goblin tribe is dead. This tribe will no longer be a threat to the citizens of Cyrodiil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE12_LOG7: The shaman of the White Skin tribe was Sir Alain's brother, Jim. Therefore, this tribe is unlikely to pose much of a threat to the citizens of Cyrodiil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE12_LOG8: Sir Alain Uruk has given me the task of ridding Cyrodiil of all of its goblin tribes. He gave me the names of the seven known tribes: the White Skin, the Bloody Hand, the Rock Biter, the Skull Breaker, the Dust Eater, the Three Feather, and the Sharp Tooth. Unfortunately, he wasn't able to give me the exact locations of their lairs.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE13_LOG0: Sir Alain Uruk rewarded me for ridding Cyrodiil of the goblin threat. He made me a Knight Errant of the Dragonborne, and rewarded me with an enchanted ring.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE13_LOG1: Sir Alain Uruk rewarded me for ridding Cyrodiil of the goblin threat. He made me a Knight Errant of the Dragonborne, and gave me a powerful staff and an enchanted ring.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE13_LOG2: Sir Alain Uruk rewarded me for ridding Cyrodiil of the goblin threat. He made me a Knight Errant of the Dragonborne, and gave me a magnificent bow and an enchanted ring.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE13_LOG3: Sir Alain Uruk rewarded me for ridding Cyrodiil of the goblin threat. He made me a Knight Errant of the Dragonborne, and gave me a magnificent mace and an enchanted ring.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE13_LOG4: Sir Alain Uruk rewarded me for ridding Cyrodiil of the goblin threat. He made me a Knight Errant of the Dragonborne, and gave me a magnificent sword and an enchanted ring.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE14_LOG0: Sir Alain has hinted that there is still more work to be done to rid Cyrodiil of the goblin menace. He mentioned that there are groups of goblins across Cyrodiil that are not affiliated with any tribe.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE15_LOG0: I gave Sir Alain the totem staff that I recovered from a goblin lair, and he rewarded me with 500 gold. He will also reward me for any additional totems I recover bring him, as well as a bonus if I bring him seven totems.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE15_LOG1: I gave Sir Alain the totem staff that I recovered from a goblin lair, and he rewarded me with 500 gold. He also gave me a bonus of 1500 gold for recovering all seven totems.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE16_LOG0: Sir Alain will pay me a bounty of 10 gold for every goblin shield I bring to him.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE17_LOG0: I have killed a number of non-tribal goblin shamans. Perhaps this will be sufficient for Sir Alain. I should go and see him in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE18_LOG0: Sir Alain Uruk rewarded me for my further efforts to wipe out the goblin menace. He made me a Knight Commander of the Dragonborne, and gave me a magnificent amulet.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE18_LOG1: Sir Alain Uruk rewarded me for my further efforts to wipe out the goblin menace. He made me a Knight Commander of the Dragonborne, and gave me a magnificent suit of light armor.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE18_LOG2: Sir Alain Uruk rewarded me for my further efforts to wipe out the goblin menace. He made me a Knight Commander of the Dragonborne, and gave me a magnificent suit of heavy armor.
NewStrings.LOC_SC_zOOODragonborne_PromotedKnight: I have been promoted to Knight Errant
NewStrings.LOC_SC_zOOODragonborne_PromotedCommander: I have been promoted to Knight Commander
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE0: After finding a pair of holy swords, a voice spoke within my own mind. It asked me to what end I would use their weapons, but I could not find the right answer. What is the secret behind these mysterious and powerful weapons?
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE1: I have discovered Melus Petilius' weapons. These are the weapons he wielded during his Paladin years. They surge with blessings of the Holy. Their power calls to me, yet I cannot lift them from the ground. An arcane enchantment, or some form of supernatural chains, binds the swords to their resting place. Perhaps I will find the way to retrieve them from Vena's grave, should Melus agree, one way or another, to my will. Even if he remains silent about this, I may find more about this mystery by inspecting his home.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE2: I have read Melus Petilius' book. In it, he confesses the deep wounds the death of his wife, Vena, caused in his spirit. With a solemn oath, he swore before the Nine to never again raise his arm in anger and violence. As a sign of their acknowledgement, they bound by holy power Melus' weapons to Vena's Grave. Melus only hints at the possibility that, were he to prove his dedication by once more defeating hordes of evil and regain his Heaven's Fury armor, the gods may lift their seals upon the swords. I must seek the armor in Lost Boy Caves, Fort Horunn, Fingerbowl Caves, Shattered Scales, Bawn and, worst of all, Lipsand Tarn
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE3: I have been given the chance to prove my worth. The Nine agreed to my answer, and I am now entitled to claim Melus Petilius' armor wherever I may find it. I think it will not be easy. I have a feeling that the Nine will test more than my strength. They will ascertain the purity of my soul before I can become their Paladin.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE4: I have recovered one piece of Melus Petilius' Heaven's Fury armor. There should be five more to go.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE5: I have recovered one more piece of Melus Petilius' Heaven's Fury armor. There should be four more to go.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE6: I have recovered one more piece of Melus Petilius' Heaven's Fury armor. There should be three more to go.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE7: I have recovered one more piece of Melus Petilius' Heaven's Fury armor. There should be two more to go.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE8: I have recovered one piece of Melus Petilius' Heaven's Fury armor. There should be one more to go.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE9: I have recovered the last piece of Melus Petilius' Heaven's Fury armor. It is time to return before Vena's grave to claim my hard-earned prize. I only hope that these feats have proven my worth before the Nine to act as their Paladin Champion.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE1: I have finally earned my right to wield the swords. The Nine smile upon me. I shall, as their Paladin Champion, prove that their gift could not have found a more worthy vanquisher of evil. As I lifted the swords from Vena's grave, I felt a shiver through my spine. The Nine have released their previous Champion. Both Vena and Melus' spirit are now free to journey together in the afterlife.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE0_LOG0: I have read an official warning posted in Bruma. The captain of the guard, Burd, has issued a call to the citizens of the town. He warns them against the recently heightened activity of Skyrim bandit clans. He notes that their level of organization seems out of the ordinary. They usually act without co-ordination. I have discovered a bandit camp from the Ice Dragon clan, but I did not find any useful knowledge there. Maybe I missed something? I should seek information that may help me figure out their designs.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE0_LOG1: I have read an official warning posted in Bruma. The captain of the guard, Burd, has issued a call to the citizens of the town. He warns them against the recently heightened activity of Skyrim bandit clans. He notes that their level of organization seems out of the ordinary. They usually act without co-ordination. Vyka's note to Marlek reveals that there is indeed an allegiance between clans, and that their plans extend far and wide. I should seek any information that may help me figure out their weaknesses.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE0_LOG2: I have read an official warning posted in Bruma. The captain of the guard, Burd, has issued a call to the citizens of the town. He warns them against the recently heightened activity of Skyrim bandit clans. He notes that their level of organization seems out of the ordinary. They usually act without co-ordination. The information I found in a nearby camp, points to more enclaves of bandits. It may be wise to investigate those too. Still, I should not discard the possibility that the locals may have more to reveal.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE0_LOG3: I have read an official warning posted in Bruma. The captain of the guard, Burd, has issued a call to the citizens of the town. He warns them against the recently heightened activity of Skyrim bandit clans. He notes that their level of organization seems out of the ordinary. They usually act without co-ordination. This warning corroborates what Blade Ferrus reported to Steffan at Cloud Ruler Temple. The information I found in a nearby camp points to more enclaves of bandits. It may be wise to investigate those too.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE0_LOG4: I have read an official warning posted in Bruma. The captain of the guard, Burd, has issued a call to the citizens of the town. He warns them against the recently heightened activity of Skyrim bandit clans. Interestingly, he notes that their level of organization seems out of the ordinary. They usually act without co-ordination. This warning corroborates what Blade Ferrus reported to Steffan, captain of the Blades at Cloud Ruler Temple. I may find more information investigating the bandits, and their camps, themselves.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE0_LOG5: I have read an official warning posted in Bruma. The captain of the guard, Burd, has issued a call to the citizens of the town. He warns them against the recently heightened activity of Skyrim bandit clans. Interestingly, he notes that their level of organization seems out of the ordinary. They usually act without co-ordination. Perhaps I can find more information about the reasons for their increase in attacks and their greater level of organization.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE1_LOG0: I have read a Blades' report about Skyrim bandit clans descending into Bruma County. Blade Ferrus informs Captain Steffan that the Blades' scouts have indentified two of these clans--the Arctic Wolves and the Three Wolves--I have found the unidentified group and their leader: Marlek of the Ice Dragon Clan. Ferrus is alarmed at their level of organization because these Nordic clans tend to be at odds against each other. He may be correct in speculating that Marlek's clan could be behind their alliance.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE1_LOG1: I have read a Blades' report about Skyrim bandit clans descending into Bruma County. Blade Ferrus informs Captain Steffan that the Blades' scouts have indentified two of these clans--the Arctic Wolves and the Three Wolves--I have found the unidentified group and their leader: Marlek of the Ice Dragon Clan. Ferrus is alarmed at their level of organization because these Nordic clans tend to be at odds against each other. I confirmed from Vyka's letter that the unidentified clan, under Marlek, forged their alliance.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE1_LOG2: I have read a Blades' report about Skyrim bandit clans descending into Bruma County. Blade Ferrus informs Captain Steffan that the Blades' scouts have indentified two of these clans--the Arctic Wolves and the Three Wolves. I learned that the unidentified group is called Ice Dragon Clan. Ferrus is alarmed at their level of organization because these Nordic clans tend to be at odds against each other. He speculates that the unidentified clan may be behind their alliance. I may find more information in their camp.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE1_LOG3: I have read a Blades' report about Skyrim bandit clans descending into Bruma County. Blade Ferrus informs Captain Steffan that the Blades' scouts have indentified two of these clans--the Arctic Wolves and the Three Wolves. I learned that the unidentified group is called Ice Dragon Clan. Ferrus is alarmed at their level of organization, just as Burd in Bruma reasoned, because these Nordic clans tend to be at odds against each other. He speculates that the unidentified clan may be behind their alliance.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE1_LOG4: I have read a Blades' report about Skyrim bandit clans descending into Bruma County. Blade Ferrus informs Captain Steffan that the Blades' scouts have indentified two of these clans--the Arctic Wolves and the Three Wolves--but that they do not recognize the markings of a third group. Ferrus is alarmed at their level of organization, just as Burd in Bruma reasoned, because these Nordic clans tend to be at odds against each other. He speculates that the unidentified clan may be behind their alliance.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE1_LOG5: I have read a Blades' report about Skyrim bandit clans descending into Bruma County. Blade Ferrus informs Captain Steffan that the Blades' scouts have indentified two of these clans--the Arctic Wolves and the Three Wolves--but that they do not recognize the markings of a third group. Ferrus is alarmed at their level of organization because these Nordic clans tend to be at odds against each other. He speculates that the unidentified clan may be behind their alliance.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE2_LOG0: In a Skyrim bandit camp near Cloud Ruler Temple I found a note written by a bandit, from a clan represented by three wolves' heads. In it, the bandit laments the actions of Vyka, leader of the Arctic Wolves, who was manipulating Marlek, the leader of the Ice Dragon clan. The message correctly notes, as I confirmed, that his camp lies northwest from the Hermaeus Mora's shrine. Perhaps Vyka's camp lies on the eastern region of the mountains, north of Dragon Claw Rock and the Silver Road.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE2_LOG1: In a Skyrim bandit camp near Cloud Ruler Temple I found a note written by a bandit, from a clan represented by three wolves' heads. In it, the bandit laments the actions of Vyka, leader of the Arctic Wolves, who was manipulating Marlek, the leader of the Ice Dragon clan. The message correctly notes that his camp lied northwest from the Hermaeus Mora's shrine. As I also confirmed, Vyka's camp did lie on the eastern region of the mountains, north of Dragon Claw Rock and the Silver Road.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE2_LOG2: In a Skyrim bandit camp near Cloud Ruler Temple I found a note written by a bandit, from a clan represented by three wolves' heads. In it, the bandit laments the actions of Vyka, leader of the Arctic Wolves, who was manipulating Marlek, the leader of the Ice Dragon clan. The message notes that his camp lies somewhere northwest from the Hermaeus Mora's shrine. As I confirmed, Vyka's camp did lie on the eastern region of the mountains, north of Dragon Claw Rock and the Silver Road.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE2_LOG3: In a Skyrim bandit camp near Cloud Ruler Temple I found a note written by a bandit, probably from a clan represented by three wolves' heads. In it, the bandit laments the actions of Vyka, a woman who apparently is manipulating Marlek, the leader of the Ice Dragon clan. The message notes that his camp lies somewhere northwest from the Hermaeus Mora's shrine. It is probable that Vyka's camp lies on the eastern region of the mountains, north of Dragon Claw Rock and the Silver Road.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE2_LOG4: In a Skyrim bandit camp near Cloud Ruler Temple I found a note written by a bandit from the Three Wolves' Clan--as discovered by Blade Ferrus. In it, the bandit laments the actions of Vyka, a woman who apparently is manipulating Marlek, the leader of the Ice Dragon clan. The message notes that his camp lies somewhere northwest from the Hermaeus Mora's shrine. NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE2_LOG5: In a Skyrim bandit camp near Cloud Ruler Temple I found a note written by a bandit from the Three Wolves' Clan--as discovered by Blade Ferrus. The message notes that his camp lies somewhere northwest from the Hermaeus Mora's shrine. It is probable that Vyka's camp lies on the eastern region of the mountains, north of Dragon Claw Rock and the Silver Road.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE2_LOG6: In a Skyrim bandit camp near Cloud Ruler Temple I found a note written by a bandit, probably from a clan represented by three wolves' heads. In it, the bandit laments the actions of Vyka, a woman who apparently is manipulating Marlek, the leader of the Ice Dragon clan. The message notes that his camp lies somewhere northwest from the Hermaeus Mora's shrine. It is probable that Vyka's camp lies on the eastern region of the mountains, north of Dragon Claw Rock and the Silver Road.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE3_LOG0: I have read a letter from Vyka addressed to Marlek. She was clearly cunning, using her influence over him to steer his decisions at her will. Marlek had the strength and charisma to join several clans together, Vyka's among them. A bond between them was then born. I infer that she was persuasive and attractive in order to move Marlek to expand his original Bruma raiding plans even further south. I wonder how Vyka's defeat at my hands will influence Marlek's decisions and his want to avenge her.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE3_LOG1: I have read a letter from Vyka addressed to Marlek. She clearly is cunning, using her influence over him to steer his decisions at her will--just as the betrayal note of the Skyrim bandits revealed. This also answers Ferrus' and Burd's question: Marlek had the strength and charisma to join several clans together, Vyka's among them. A bond between them was then born. I infer that she must be persuasive and attractive in order to move Marlek to expand his original Bruma raiding plans even further south.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE3_LOG2: I have read a letter from Vyka addressed to Marlek. She clearly is cunning, using her influence over him to steer his decisions at her will--just as the betrayal note of the Skyrim bandits revealed. This also answers Blade Ferrus' question: Marlek had the strength and charisma to join several clans together, Vyka's among them. A bond between them was then born. I infer that she must be persuasive and attractive in order to move Marlek to expand his original raiding plans even further south of Cyrodiil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE3_LOG3: I have read a letter from Vyka addressed to Marlek. She clearly is cunning, using her influence over him to steer his decisions at her will--just as the betrayal note of the Skyrim bandits revealed. Apparently, Marlek had the strength and charisma to join several clans together, Vyka's among them. A bond between them was then born. I infer that she must be persuasive and attractive in order to move Marlek to expand his original raiding plans even further south. This is the source of Bruma's trouble and Burd's worry.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE3_LOG4: I have read a letter from a certain Vyka addressed to Marlek. She is clearly a cunning woman, using her influence over him to steer his decisions at her will. Apparently, Marlek had the strength and charisma to join several clans together, Vyka's among them. A relationship between them was then born. I infer that she must be persuasive and attractive in order to move Marlek to expand his original raiding plans even further south. This answers many questions for which Blade Ferrus and Captain Burd had no answer.
